_note: Real per-season data, 1994-2025, all sides of the ball. Sources by span: floridagators.com official Sidearm stats + roster payloads (2006-2025; pipeline draft cross-validated player-by-player against Sports-Reference season tables, which exposed and repaired ~30 Sidearm bio-mislinks - e.g. Vernon Hargreaves' 2013 freshman line filed under a kicker's bio and Trey Burton's 2010 14-TD season under a walk-on - each repaired/reassigned row cites the SR season page it was verified against; SR carries no PBU before 2013, so official PBU stands there). 1996-2005 come from Wayback-archived OFFICIAL sources: gatorzone.com per-season history pages (final 1996 offense; 1998 cumulative + 11-game defense), the gatorzone stats PDFs (team.pdf/defe.pdf: 1999 through the SEC Championship), history/<year>/team.pdf and stats.pdf packages (2000-2005 finals incl. bowls where published; 2001 through the SECCG), and the notes/final/2002.pdf defensive tables - every parsed table validated against its printed totals row (column sums exact). 1994-1995 are transcribed from the digitized official Florida media guides (1995 + 1996 guides, Internet Archive item 01-florida-gators) at full page-scan resolution, validated by the guides' own per-row BP checksum (BP = TTFL+FH+FF+FR+BLK+PD+INT), column totals (UT/AT/TT exact to the printed 635/462/1097 and 605/440/1045), the printed AVG columns, and the interception-returns cross-table; guide scope is regular season incl. the SEC Championship (bowls excluded, as printed). UF credited fractional sacks in halves AND thirds (Kevin Carter's 11.8 = 11 5/6 QBS in 1994 is confirmed by the archived UF record book). 1997 (11-game regular season) and the final 1996 defense come from the 1998/1997 media guides under the same checksum discipline. Positions: verified player-by-player with citations (floridagators.com bios/rosters back to 1996, Wikipedia, draft profiles); coarse roster codes (DL/DB/STAR/BUCK) were never defaulted; OL/K/P/LS are not draftable and were dropped (incl. honored specialists: Maurkice Pouncey, Chas Henry, Johnny Townsend); two same-name humans (the David Reeses) disambiguated via SR's own split rows. honors[] is per-season and award-first, derived programmatically from Wikipedia wikitext (the Florida All-Americans list page with its consensus/unanimous color legend, per-year All-SEC team articles 1994-2025 with AP/Coaches levels - highest level kept, national award winner tables, and the SEC individual awards page); honors whose winner has no dataset row (OL/K/P) are recorded in the build ledger, not attached. redshirtYears is coverage bookkeeping, not verified biography.

honors: SEC Offensive Player of the Year (2008)
SEC Offensive Player of the Year (2009)
